mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This command adds the Cluster API Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace, which is required for managing clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace capi-system

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

```

> Create a namespace for Cluster API.
> Install Cluster API with the specified version.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Provider
steps.description:
This step installs a specific provider for Cluster API, which is necessary for managing the infrastructure where the clusters will be deployed.

```bash

helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

```

> Install the AWS provider for Cluster API.
commands: helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

kubectl get crd

```

> Verify that all Cluster API pods are running.
> Check that the necessary Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and may result in data loss for any clusters managed by it. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

helm uninstall cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete namespace capi-system

```

> Uninstall the Cluster API components.
> Uninstall the Cluster API AWS provider.
> Delete the namespace to clean up resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup any important configurations and data. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Drain the nodes if necessary.
> Upgrade the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n capi-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data
helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRDs are already installed or if there are permission issues.

**Fix:**
Check existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd' and ensure you have the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one of the Cluster API components is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n capi-system' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Kubernetes cluster is not reachable or if kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig with 'kubectl config view' and ensure the cluster is up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind 'Cluster' in version 'cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the required CRDs for Cluster API are not installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CRDs are installed by running 'kubectl get crd'. If not, reinstall the Cluster API.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for custom resource definitions and added new features for cluster management.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.12.4, no specific migration steps are required.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n capi-system' showing all pods in the Running state.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5
helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
